# Copilot instructions ‚Äî zebacodes.com

Purpose: give an AI coding agent the minimal, high-value information needed to be immediately productive in this repository.

1) Repository snapshot
- Scan time: 2026-05-08
- Scan result: no source files or common build/config files (package.json, pyproject.toml, Dockerfile, etc.) were found. Because of that, this file contains a short, actionable template and explicit TODOs for repository-specific details.

2) High-level goals for the agent
- Prioritize locating build and test entrypoints, CI configs, and any deploy descriptors (.github/workflows, netlify.toml, vercel.json, Dockerfile).
- If files are missing, open an issue or add a short README entry before implementing non-trivial features.

3) Repository-specific instructions (REPLACE these placeholders)
- Primary language / framework: <fill in: e.g. Next.js / Node / Python Flask / Hugo>
- How to run locally: <fill in exact commands, e.g. `npm install && npm run dev`>
- How to run tests: <fill in exact command, e.g. `npm test` or `pytest -q`>
- Lint / formatting command: <fill in, e.g. `npm run lint` or `ruff .`>
- Production build & deploy command: <fill in, e.g. `npm run build && vercel --prod`>

4) What to look for first (checklist for the agent)
- Is there a `package.json`, `pyproject.toml`, `requirements.txt`, `Cargo.toml` or similar? Use it to derive build/test commands.
- Look for `.github/workflows/**` to see CI steps and environment variables.
- Look for `netlify.toml`, `vercel.json`, `Dockerfile`, `Procfile`, `serverless.yml` to locate deploy logic.
- Search for `.env`, `.env.example`, or `*.secret` patterns to learn required env vars (do NOT attempt to fill secrets).
- Locate top-level README.md or docs/ for architecture notes.

5) Coding patterns & conventions to prefer
- Keep changes small and focused: open PRs with 1 feature or 1 bug fix + tests.
- Prefer existing conventions if present: e.g. if `scripts` in `package.json` use `npm`/`pnpm` consistently.
- If CI uses a specific Node/Python version in workflows, match that locally and in dev containers.

6) Files and examples the agent should inspect when present
- `package.json` ‚Äî scripts, dependencies, engines
- `tsconfig.json` / `jsconfig.json` ‚Äî module resolution and path aliases
- `pyproject.toml` / `requirements.txt` ‚Äî test and lint tooling
- `.github/workflows/*.yml` ‚Äî CI steps and secrets usage
- `src/` or `app/` ‚Äî application entrypoints and routing
- `netlify.toml` / `vercel.json` / `Dockerfile` ‚Äî deploy behavior

7) Safe edit rules (do not change without human confirmation)
- Do not modify CI secrets, `.env` or any file that looks like a secret store.
- Avoid large refactors without opening an issue and getting human review.
- When changing build pipelines, run CI locally or in a draft PR first.

8) If you need to add tests
- Add a small, focused test that reproduces the issue or verifies behavior. Put tests next to code (project convention) or follow test/ or tests/ existing layout.

9) Reporting back
- For any non-trivial change (new test, build config change, refactor), include in the PR description:
  - What you changed and why
  - How to run and verify locally (exact commands)
  - CI results and any new environment variables required

10) How you can help update this file
- Please replace the placeholders in section 3 with accurate commands and a short architecture summary. When those are present, AI agents will use them directly and reduce follow-up questions.

Questions or missing information: open an issue named "repo: missing run/test/build instructions" and paste the contents of section 3.
